source_status.indexeddb_specification: W3C Working Draft, 13 August 2025; work in progress

finding: IndexedDB permits multiple simultaneous clients, defines versionchange and abnormal close behavior, and distinguishes committed transactions from recovery after interruption. A browser harness therefore needs separate fields for capability support, transaction completion, replay rejection, interruption injection, reopen observation, and persistence outcome rather than one pass flag.
design_implication: Cross-browser results are accepted only through a fail-closed evidence contract covering Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit across contention, versionchange/forced close, bfcache restore, quota pressure, and abrupt termination recovery. A completed transaction remains persistence_not_proven unless an interruption is injected and the record is observed after reopen.

verification.method: isolated Node test execution using byte-identical pre-commit source

evidence_strength: committed validator and deterministic unit tests; no real-browser observations yet
uncertainty: Browser automation can exercise page and context lifecycle, but abrupt operating-system process termination and storage flush behavior may require platform-specific runners beyond normal graceful context closure.
falsification_route: Reject the contract if a scenario can be marked pass while its defining observation is absent.
Revise browser or scenario enums when an official runner cannot represent the required engine or lifecycle event.
Treat persistence as disproven for a tested interruption when the committed acceptance record is absent after reopen.
Treat two-tab contention as failed if both clients accept the same opaque identifier.
privacy_boundary: No private chat, health data, credentials, account identifiers, or user records are included.
